title: The Fourteenth Floor

jokes.text: This will come as very good news to my grandmother who needs a spotter to pick up a spoon.
jokes.setup: News study about elderly people benefiting from weight lifting
jokes.punchline: Absurd contrast - grandmother so frail she needs help lifting a spoon
jokes.explanation: Classic monologue joke with good setup/payoff structure and vivid visual imagery

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This will come as very good news to my grandmother who needs a spotter to pick up a spoon.

jokes.text: listen, when you live in the bay area, it's not easy to say 'bay area man.'
jokes.setup: Larry stumbling over pronunciation: 'A bear... a bay... a bay area man...'
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on the difficulty of saying the phrase he just butchered
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating recovery from a flub, typical talk show host move

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: listen, when you live in the bay area, it's not easy to say 'bay area man.'

jokes.text: The intoxicated man later tried to convince police he was one of those new, crazy, flesh-eating viruses.
jokes.setup: Man charged with biting exotic dancer
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse claiming to be a flesh-eating virus
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation from drunk behavior to absurd medical claim

dialogue.line: The intoxicated man later tried to convince police he was one of those new, crazy, flesh-eating viruses.

jokes.text: When asked to comment on this, a white house aide said, 'please go away. If she finds out I talked to you, she'll hurt me.'
jokes.setup: Poll says Hillary Clinton has too much power
jokes.punchline: White House aide's terrified, pleading response
jokes.explanation: Good political joke playing on Hillary's reputation for toughness

dialogue.line: When asked to comment on this, a white house aide said, 'please go away. If she finds out I talked to you, she'll hurt me.'

jokes.text: 'you know, you're too old, you can't see, and you're a woman,' which, by coincidence, is exactly the same excuse Bill Clinton used to get out.
jokes.setup: Hillary Clinton rejected from Marines for being too old, blind, and female
jokes.punchline: Bill Clinton used same excuse to avoid military service
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with perfect misdirection and timing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: 'you know, you're too old, you can't see, and you're a woman,' which, by coincidence, is exactly the same excuse Bill Clinton used to get out.

jokes.text: It's about a father and his 5 wacky kids. It's about a father and 4 kids. It doesn't even add up to 6, and yet it's called... the 6 of us. They're too cheap to hire the sixth guy.
jokes.setup: Network show called 'The 6 of Us'
jokes.punchline: Can't count to 6, too cheap to hire sixth actor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant industry satire about network stupidity and cheapness - very Larry Sanders

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It's about a father and his 5 wacky kids. It's about a father and 4 kids. It doesn't even add up to 6, and yet it's called... the 6 of us. They're too cheap to hire the sixth guy.

jokes.text: They said, 'well, that's... hopefully, people watch every week to wonder who the other one is.'
jokes.setup: Larry points out the math doesn't add up to 6
jokes.punchline: Network's idiotic response that viewers will tune in to solve the mystery
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that shows network executive stupidity. Great character work in the executive's voice.

dialogue.line: They said, 'well, that's... hopefully, people watch every week to wonder who the other one is.'

jokes.text: I actually believe that you could replace all of our network executives with chimps and, other than the, uh, hygiene factor going up, why, no one would know the difference.
jokes.setup: Could replace network executives with chimps
jokes.punchline: Only difference would be improved hygiene
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie line - gruff industry veteran insulting executives, perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I actually believe that you could replace all of our network executives with chimps and, other than the, uh, hygiene factor going up, why, no one would know the difference.

jokes.text: Great show, great guests tomorrow, you go home, have a glass of wine, whack off. I'll see you tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Post-show critique starting normally
jokes.punchline: Casually includes masturbation in evening plans
jokes.explanation: Peak Artie - inappropriate but delivered so matter-of-factly it's brilliant

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Great show, great guests tomorrow, you go home, have a glass of wine, whack off. I'll see you tomorrow.

jokes.text: Dr. Don! I didn't recognize you with my pants on!
jokes.setup: Mistaking network exec for vitamin shot doctor
jokes.punchline: Implies he's usually pantless during medical visits
jokes.explanation: Awkward overshare in professional setting - classic cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Dr. Don! I didn't recognize you with my pants on!

jokes.text: If you stand up too fast, you'll get hit in the head by the boom.
jokes.setup: Hank says he's 'steamed' about something
jokes.punchline: Artie thinks he means literally hit by boom mic
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke showing behind-the-scenes TV reality vs emotional drama

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: If you stand up too fast, you'll get hit in the head by the boom.

jokes.text: His heart's in the right place, but he keeps his brain in a box at home.
jokes.setup: Standard 'heart in right place' setup about Hank
jokes.punchline: Brain stored separately at home like seasonal decorations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of cliché with perfect visual absurdity and economy

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: His heart's in the right place, but he keeps his brain in a box at home.

jokes.text: I gave him some candy and a balloon and sent him on his merry way.
jokes.setup: Network executive meeting
jokes.punchline: Treated like a child at a doctor's office
jokes.explanation: Artie's condescending dismissal of young network exec, perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I gave him some candy and a balloon and sent him on his merry way.

jokes.text: Or maybe we say that you've discovered the network family tree and you pull out this dead little Bush or somethin'.
jokes.setup: Joking about network 'family' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Visual pun - dead bush as literal family tree branch
jokes.explanation: Decent visual pun but not particularly sharp or memorable

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Or maybe we say that you've discovered the network family tree and you pull out this dead little Bush or somethin'.

jokes.text: I believe that's the same message on, uh, Michael Jackson's answering machine.
jokes.setup: Circus ringmaster calling 'boys and girls, children of all ages'
jokes.punchline: Implies Michael Jackson has inappropriate interest in children
jokes.explanation: Topical but somewhat cheap shot at Jackson - relies on scandal rather than wit

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I believe that's the same message on, uh, Michael Jackson's answering machine.

jokes.text: Not always easy for the network to find the chair.
jokes.setup: Young network exec sits down as guest
jokes.punchline: Implies network executives are incompetent at basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Good hostile host moment - Larry being passive-aggressively insulting

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Not always easy for the network to find the chair.

jokes.text: Well, I was in nursery school.
jokes.setup: Asked what he did before joining network in '87
jokes.punchline: Deadpan admission he was a toddler
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery highlighting how young network executives are

dialogue.speaker: Daniel Palmer
dialogue.line: Well, I was in nursery school.

jokes.text: Oh, so you write as well?
jokes.setup: Network exec wrote episodes of terrible show 'The 6 of Us'
jokes.punchline: Larry's fake impressed reaction to admitting bad writing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier 6 of Us bit, Larry's sarcastic hosting style

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, so you write as well?

jokes.text: Well, I only know what's on until 8:30 because that's my bedtime.
jokes.setup: Asked about network's fall lineup
jokes.punchline: Admits child-like early bedtime
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - young exec continues childish admissions

dialogue.speaker: Daniel Palmer
dialogue.line: Well, I only know what's on until 8:30 because that's my bedtime.

jokes.text: Manson had a family.
jokes.setup: Network exec saying 'we're a family'
jokes.punchline: Darkly pointing out families can be dysfunctional/murderous
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - perfect economy and subversion of corporate speak with historical reference

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Manson had a family.

jokes.text: Fine. I'll write it up. Have it upstairs by the end of the day.
jokes.setup: Network wants Larry to apologize on-air
jokes.punchline: Artie offers to write Larry's personal apology like a memo
jokes.explanation: Great callback - Artie repeats exact same response, showing his practical approach

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Fine. I'll write it up. Have it upstairs by the end of the day.

jokes.text: Fine. I'll write it up. Have it upstairs by the end of the day.
jokes.setup: Executive clarifies they want on-air apology from Larry specifically
jokes.punchline: Artie repeats exact same response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Artie will handle everything regardless of what they actually want. Perfect character stubbornness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Fine. I'll write it up. Have it upstairs by the end of the day.

jokes.text: The network respects you too much to play that kind of game.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if they threatened to move the show later
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic denial while they literally just did that
jokes.explanation: Good Artie deflection - obvious lie delivered with straight face.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The network respects you too much to play that kind of game.

jokes.text: I've been makin' waves since the midwife tugged me out of my mother's underside.
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting Artie is afraid of making waves
jokes.punchline: Colorful birth imagery showing lifelong troublemaking
jokes.explanation: Vintage Artie - crude but poetic language defending his rebellious nature

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I've been makin' waves since the midwife tugged me out of my mother's underside.

jokes.text: we'll kick 'em in the ass.
jokes.setup: We'll lay apology at their feet, when they bend over to pick it up
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of kicking them while bent over
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with classic slapstick payoff

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: we'll kick 'em in the ass.

jokes.text: Who the fuck do you think I am, Mr. Chips?
jokes.setup: Larry asking if network exec is doing homework
jokes.punchline: Artie rejecting comparison to caring teacher character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - profane rejection of nurturing role with literary reference

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Who the fuck do you think I am, Mr. Chips?

jokes.text: If only the arabs and israelis would give cup-a-soup.
jokes.setup: Hank bringing cup-a-soup as peace offering
jokes.punchline: Suggesting instant soup could solve Middle East conflict
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation from TV show apology to international relations

dialogue.line: If only the arabs and israelis would give cup-a-soup.

jokes.text: Oh, God! Aiyeee!
jokes.setup: Hank trying to sneak past receptionist with flowers and soup
jokes.punchline: Trips over cable and injures himself
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy - Hank's clumsiness derailing the peace mission

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, God! Aiyeee!

jokes.text: I get a bad tooth, he goes to Maui.
jokes.setup: Phone call from dentist
jokes.punchline: Dentist unavailable when patient needs him most
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie complaint - personal inconvenience delivered with gruff humor

dialogue.line: I get a bad tooth, he goes to Maui.

jokes.text: Get off my ass. I'm hungry.
jokes.setup: Paula commenting on Artie eating ice cream
jokes.punchline: Artie's defensive response about eating habits
jokes.explanation: Typical Artie moment - unnecessarily aggressive about minor comment

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Get off my ass. I'm hungry.

jokes.text: I heard he's found the lord.
jokes.setup: Hank complaining about losing ringmaster role to Gavin MacLeod
jokes.punchline: Casual religious explanation for career success
jokes.explanation: Mild Hollywood commentary on born-again celebrities

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I heard he's found the lord.

jokes.text: If I was 10 years on that boat, I'd find the lord, too.
jokes.setup: Reference to Gavin MacLeod finding religion
jokes.punchline: Love Boat reference - being stuck on cruise ship would drive anyone to God
jokes.explanation: Good callback with TV industry reference - Hank showing actual wit

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: If I was 10 years on that boat, I'd find the lord, too.

jokes.text: Gavin mccloud doesn't know one fucking ring from another.
jokes.setup: Hank's frustration about losing circus job
jokes.punchline: Professional jealousy with circus expertise claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank - taking circus expertise way too seriously

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Gavin mccloud doesn't know one fucking ring from another.

jokes.text: I already had my... lunch. I had a ham and bourbon sammich.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about going out for lunch
jokes.punchline: Artie's alcoholic 'lunch' with childish pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Artie's drinking problem delivered with innocent mispronunciation

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I already had my... lunch. I had a ham and bourbon sammich.

jokes.text: Is this the ham talking?
jokes.setup: Artie acting belligerent after bourbon lunch
jokes.punchline: Larry's dry response to obvious intoxication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - acknowledging the drinking without confronting it directly

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Is this the ham talking?

jokes.text: The little guy with the palsy, right?
jokes.setup: Artie mentioning Ernie who cuts hair for news department
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate description of disabled stylist
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor relying on disability - more cringe than clever

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The little guy with the palsy, right?

jokes.text: I hope you like it short and uneven.
jokes.setup: Larry will get haircut from guy with palsy
jokes.punchline: Obvious result of shaky hands cutting hair
jokes.explanation: Logical follow-up to previous joke. Functional but predictable punchline.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I hope you like it short and uneven.

jokes.text: No. I'm the goat. They're gonna fire me, see? They say that I can't handle you, so they're gonna bring in their own man.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if they'll just fire him
jokes.punchline: Artie explains he's the scapegoat who will be replaced
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Artie's vulnerability and understanding of network politics.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No. I'm the goat. They're gonna fire me, see? They say that I can't handle you, so they're gonna bring in their own man.

jokes.text: Before we get any further into tonight's show, I would like to say that... we've been doing some joking about the network the last couple of weeks, and that is my responsibility. I take full responsibility for it, and if I so overstepped my boundaries and insulted the network in any way, I apologize, and I would like to be a member of the network family again...
jokes.setup: Larry giving seemingly sincere apology to network
jokes.punchline: The obvious insincerity and forced nature of corporate apology
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on forced TV apologies - the insincerity is the joke

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Before we get any further into tonight's show, I would like to say that... we've been doing some joking about the network the last couple of weeks, and that is my responsibility. I take full responsibility for it, and if I so overstepped my boundaries and insulted the network in any way, I apologize, and I would like to be a member of the network family again...

jokes.text: that nice lady is gonna be, uh, tripping over a little thing called a subpoena.
jokes.setup: Network lady who pushed Hank was just trying to help
jokes.punchline: Hank threatening legal action over minor accident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank - escalating minor mishap into major lawsuit threat

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: that nice lady is gonna be, uh, tripping over a little thing called a subpoena.

tmdb_overview: Larry gets into a feud with the network when he makes fun of their new fall schedule on the air.
